text.title: inverted-header-content-first Failure Modes
text.summary: Three failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Putting `summary` first because "humans read summaries first".
antipattern.reason: Schema order is generation order, not display order. The model is the consumer at sample time; the human is the consumer at view time. Optimise for the model.

section.p: Symptom: summary feels disconnected from body. Root cause: confused generation vs display order. Fix: keep body first in the schema; let the frontend re-order at display time.

antipattern.description: Reordering the one-call schema to title-first because the frontend needs the title earlier.
antipattern.reason: Trades measurable accuracy loss (13 pp Castillo gap) for a UI convenience that two cheap calls solve cleanly.

section.p: Symptom: lower-quality bodies after the schema change. Root cause: UI-driven reorder. Fix: split into two SO calls — body first, then a cheap metadata call that reads the body.

antipattern.description: Schema generates a `title` field when the user already supplied a title in the prompt.
antipattern.reason: Title drift from user intent; wasted tokens.

section.p: Symptom: generated titles differ from user-supplied ones. Root cause: title in schema. Fix: pass title in the prompt; omit from schema; reference by name in body's description.
